articles.title: Dow Soars 875 Points to Record Close; S&P 500 Overcomes Broadcom-Led Tech Pullback; Oil Retreats
articles.summary: Dow surged 875 points (+1.7%) to a record close, led by healthcare (UNH +5%, MRK +5%) and financials (GS +5%). S&P 500 gained 0.4% despite Broadcom-led tech selloff. Nasdaq slipped 0.1%. Broadcom (AVGO) plunged 13% post-earnings, dragging chip stocks and CRWD. WTI crude fell ~3% to $93.20 on Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. 10Y yield eased to 4.48%. Bitcoin dropped to $63,300, lowest since February. Gold up 0.9% above $4,500. SpaceX IPO filed at $135/share, ~$75B raise, $1.75T valuation. Beige Book flagged inflation risks from Iran war fuel costs.

articles.title: Broadcom Q2 Earnings: Beat on EPS, Miss on Revenue, AI Guidance Unchanged — Stock Plunges 15%
articles.summary: AVGO Q2 adjusted EPS $2.44 beat by $0.04; revenue $22.19B missed $22.27B consensus. Net income $9.31B (+88% YoY). AI revenue $10.8B (doubled YoY), expected to triple to $16B in Q3. FY2026 AI guidance reiterated at 'in excess of $100B' — no raise, disappointing investors. Software revenue $7.18B missed $7.32B consensus. Q3 revenue guidance $29.4B beat estimates. Hock Tan announced shift to 'chips only' strategy. Stock plunged ~15% but analysts broadly raised PTs (JPM $580, Jefferies $550, BNP $640). Still +20% YTD.

articles.title: Strategy (MSTR) Breaks 'Never Sell' Posture: Sold 32 BTC for $2.5M, Stock Craters
articles.summary: Strategy (MSTR) sold 32 BTC for $2.5M to fund STRK preferred dividends — its first Bitcoin sale since 2022. Sale represents 0.004% of 843,706 BTC holdings but shattered the 'never sell' narrative. Stock down 31% in one month, 67% YoY, trading at ~$126. BTC at $62,350, well below Strategy's $75,699 average cost basis. Q4 2025 net loss: $17.44B unrealized on digital assets. Polymarket: 63% probability of MSCI delisting by year-end. HOLD rating at $126; watch for BTC purchase announcement June 2-8 window.

articles.title: Strategy's Bitcoin Dive Will Cost $26M/Year as STRC Preferred Dividend Set to Ratchet to 11.75%
articles.summary: Strategy's STRC preferred (currently 11.5% dividend) likely to ratchet to 11.75% as monthly VWAP fell to ~96.90 vs. 99 trigger. Would add $26M/year in interest on $10.5B outstanding. STRC trading at 94, well below $100 par — freezing new issuance and bitcoin buying power. Total annual interest/dividend burden: $1.712B (3.1% of BTC holdings). At 11.75%, STRC-financed BTC purchases unprofitable even at 10% annual BTC appreciation. MSTR down 20.5% week-to-date; BTC tumbled to ~$62,350 (lowest since Nov 2024). Next payment: ~$235M due end of June.

articles.title: May Jobs Report Preview: Consensus +80K Payrolls, Risks Skewed to Downside
articles.summary: May nonfarm payrolls consensus: +80K (vs. April's +115K). Unemployment expected at 4.3-4.4%. Downside risks substantial: Goldman +60K, Vanguard +20K, EY-Parthenon +50K. Challenger report: 97,006 planned job cuts (+16% MoM), AI-related layoffs 38,242 (highest on record). Initial jobless claims at highest since February. 'Low-hire, low-fire' stagnation narrative. Fed: zero probability of rate move at June 16-17 FOMC. Rate hike odds increasing for early 2027 if inflation stays elevated. 'More hawkish, two-sided policy statement' expected.

articles.title: ADP: Private Payrolls Rose 122,000 in May, Beating 110K Consensus
articles.summary: Private payrolls grew 122K in May, beating 110K consensus and April's 105K (revised down 4K). Strongest month since January 2025. Hiring broad-based: education/health +57K, trade/transport +36K, professional services +11K. Information services -9K (AI impact?). Small businesses +67K, large businesses +40K. Pay growth: stayers 4.4% (unchanged), switchers 6.5% (edged down). ADP chief economist: 'Hiring was more broad-based than we've seen in the last few years.' Contradicts BLS consensus weakness.

articles.title: Snowflake Surges 36% on AI Frenzy, Easing 'SaaSpocalypse' Fears
articles.summary: Snowflake shares surged 36% (best day ever) on $6B Amazon AI compute deal and earnings beat. Q2 product revenue guidance $1.415-1.420B (above $1.37B consensus). CFO: AI tools driving 'step function change' in AI revenue. Added 616 net new customers (+38% YoY); 779 customers >$1M spend. Acquired AI startup Natoma. Rally lifted SaaS peers: NOW +6%, ORCL +6%, PLTR +8%, TEAM +3%. Exception: CRM dipped on lackluster guidance. Eased fears that AI would replace traditional SaaS. SNOW up ~9% YTD after surge.

articles.title: Broadcom Analyst Roundup: Jefferies Raises PT to $550, Sees Buying Opportunity
articles.summary: 5-star Jefferies analyst Blayne Curtis (70% success rate, 41% avg return) raised AVGO PT to $550 (from $500), reiterates Buy. Argues AI growth story intact, margin headwinds temporary. Broad analyst PT raises: BNP $640, JPM $580, KeyBanc $575, Bernstein $550, Goldman $525. AVGO posted 4th consecutive double beat; revenue $68.3B TTM (+25% YoY), gross margin 77%. Stock: +38.47% YTD, +86.58% 1Y, +490% 3Y. Shift to custom AI chips pressures near-term margins but hyperscaler demand (Meta, OpenAI) ramping for 2027.

articles.title: Investment Grade Bonds Q2 2026: 5.00-5.50% All-In Yields, 80 bps Spreads Near 25-Year Lows
articles.summary: IG bond market at inflection point: all-in yields 5.00-5.50% (highest carry in decade+) but OAS at ~80 bps (near multi-decade tights). IG issuance forecast: $2.46T in 2026 (+11.8% YoY), net $945B (+30.2%). AI/data center IG debt: $300B+, hyperscalers averaging $140B annual issuance. Quality ladder compressed: BBB OAS ~100 bps vs AAA ~40 bps — only 60 bps across rating spectrum, tightest since late 1990s. Recommendation: clip coupon, don't bet on further spread compression. Favor defensives (healthcare, utilities) over cyclicals at current spreads.

articles.title: US Credit Markets Defy War Risks as Cash Drives Rally; IG Spreads at 78 bps
articles.summary: IG spreads tightened to 78 bps (near historic 73 bps low), HY at 275 bps. $1T+ corporate issuance through April (+28.2% YoY). M2 money supply up 6% YoY. Drivers: abundant liquidity from expansionary fiscal policy, Fed supporting $3T bank reserves, insurers now ~50% of demand in some segments (from 20% a decade ago). Corporate balance sheets in 'excellent health.' Risk: lower-quality HY and private credit vulnerable if growth slows. Market 'quickly gets over bad news' because of ample cash.

articles.title: Dollar Climbs for Fifth Straight Day as Treasury Yields Surge on Inflation Fears
articles.summary: DXY rose to 99.30 (fifth consecutive daily gain) as 10Y yield hit 4.595% (highest in a year). WTI crude surged to $105/bbl on Iran Strait of Hormuz blockage fears. Market priced 49.5% probability of a December rate hike (from 14.3% a week prior). EUR/USD dropped to $1.1623, USD/JPY rose to 158.74 (nearing 160 intervention level). Bond market repricing long-term inflation expectations as quick energy resolution looks unlikely.

articles.title: Bitcoin Stocks Take a Beating as BTC Sells Off Below $67K; MSTR, COIN, ASST All Down
articles.summary: BTC fell into mid-$67Ks, down 46% from October peak of $126K. MSTR -9.15%, COIN -4.23%, ASST -6.23%. Four catalysts: (1) Strategy sold 32 BTC, (2) Record ETF outflows — $3.45B over 11 consecutive sessions, (3) Mt. Gox moved 10,422 BTC (~$739M), (4) Iran suspended nuclear negotiations. Strive (ASST) bought 2,500 BTC at $74,092 avg but still fell 6.23%. Structural treasury model risk: when BTC drops, equities drop harder. MSTR dangerously near 52-week floor of $104.16.
